goal: is_countable[T](Set.singleton(x)) = (Set.singleton(x) = Set.empty_set[T] or exists(k0: Nat -> T) { forall(x0: T) { Set.singleton(x).contains(x0) implies exists(k1: Nat) { k0(k1) = x0 } } })
proof: function[T0](x0: Set[T0]) { (Set.empty_set[T0] = x0 or exists(k0: Nat -> T0) { forall(x1: T0) { not x1 ∈ x0 or exists(k1: Nat) { k0(k1) = x1 } } }) = is_countable[T0](x0) }[T](Set.singleton(x))

function(x0: Nat) { y != f(x0) }(Nat.0)

goal: exists(k0: Nat -> T) { forall(x0: T) { Set.singleton(x).contains(x0) implies exists(k1: Nat) { k0(k1) = x0 } } }
proof: function(x0: Nat -> T) { not forall(x1: T) { not x1 ∈ Set.singleton(x) or exists(k0: Nat) { x0(k0) = x1 } } }(f)

let w1(arg0: Nat -> T) -> w1_result: T satisfy { w1_result ∈ Set.singleton(x) and forall(x1: Nat) { arg0(x1) != w1_result } }

function(x0: Nat -> T) { forall(x1: Nat) { x0(x1) != w1(x0) } = true }(f)
function(x0: Nat -> T) { exists(k0: Nat) { f(k0) = w1(x0) } }(f)
let w2: Nat satisfy { f(w2) = w1(f) }
let w3(arg0: Nat -> T) -> w3_result: Nat satisfy { f(w3_result) = w1(arg0) }
